name: Waratah Super Battery
notable: Operating at 350 MW (41% of design capacity) due to catastrophic transformer failure Oct 2025. HVT3 ruptured beyond repair; HVT2 de-energised as precaution. Replacement transformer delivery Q3 2026, full 850 MW targeted end of 2026. SIPS contract ~$705M/5yr. OEM Powin filed Chapter 11 bankruptcy June 2025 — assets acquired by FlexGen Aug 2025.
operational_capacity_note: Derated from 850 MW to 350 MW since Oct 2025 transformer failure. Only HVT1 operational. Full capacity targeted end of 2026.

timeline.title: Akaysha Energy selected for NSW SIPS battery
timeline.detail: Akaysha (BlackRock Real Assets) selected by NSW govt to build 850 MW / 1,680 MWh battery at Munmorah site near Eraring. SIPS contract worth ~$705M over 5 years via Transgrid.

timeline.title: Construction begins
timeline.detail: Powin selected as battery OEM (2,592 Centipede Energy Segments). EKS Energy (Hitachi) for 288 power conversion systems. CPP as EPC. Wilson Transformer for three 350 MVA transformers (HVT1, HVT2, HVT3).

timeline.title: AEMO registration — commercial operations commenced
timeline.detail: Commencement date from AEMO registration via OpenElectricity. DUID registered as NEM's largest single battery unit.

timeline.title: First generation recorded
timeline.detail: First market dispatch data recorded in AEMO systems.

timeline.title: Battery OEM Powin files Chapter 11 bankruptcy
timeline.detail: Powin filed for Chapter 11 in US Bankruptcy Court with >$500M debt. Causes include tariffs on Chinese imports and IRA uncertainty. Long-term warranty and spare parts implications for Waratah.

timeline.title: Powin assets acquired by FlexGen
timeline.detail: FlexGen acquired Powin's assets out of Chapter 11 bankruptcy. Warranty and service continuity for Waratah to be confirmed under new ownership.

timeline.title: Partial COD — 350 MW (41% of design capacity)
timeline.detail: First 350 MW / 700 MWh commenced operations under SIPS obligations using transformer HVT1 only. HVT2 and HVT3 not yet energised. Battery operating as NEM's largest single DUID but at less than half design capacity.

timeline.title: Catastrophic transformer failure (HVT3)
timeline.detail: Wilson Transformer HVT3 suffered catastrophic internal fault 4 hours before completing final Hold Point 5 testing. Overpressure event ruptured tank wall — transformer beyond repair. HVT2 also de-energised as precaution pending inspection. Battery capped at 350 MW (HVT1 only). Estimated loss A$80M+.

timeline.title: Full plant shutdown for transformer remediation
timeline.detail: Full balance-of-plant shutdown 20 Nov to 2 Dec 2025 for HVT2 inspection and remediation works. Zero generation during shutdown period.

timeline.title: Replacement transformer manufacturing underway
timeline.detail: Wilson Transformer manufacturing replacement HVT3. Design review completed by CPP and independent consultants. Delivery to site targeted Q3 2026.

timeline.title: HVT2 remediation — return to service pending
timeline.detail: HVT2 inspection and remediation works completed. Return to service pending final testing and AEMO approval. Would restore capacity to ~700 MW (HVT1 + HVT2).

timeline.title: Full 850 MW operations targeted end of 2026
timeline.detail: All three transformers (HVT1, HVT2, HVT3) operational targeted for end of 2026. Dependent on HVT3 replacement delivery (Q3 2026) and successful commissioning.

eis_specs.document_title: Waratah Super Battery — State Significant Development Assessment (NSW IPC SSD-2022-0195)
eis_specs.connection_substation_name: Tomago 330 kV substation (TransGrid), Hunter Valley NSW
eis_specs.connection_distance_note: ~2 km from BESS site to TransGrid Tomago 330 kV substation, Hunter Valley NSW.
eis_specs.connection_augmentation: TransGrid Tomago substation upgrade for 850 MW BESS. NSW IPC SSD assessment included detailed TransGrid connection feasibility. Critical grid support as Hunter Valley coal plants retire.
eis_specs.notes: 850 MW / 1,680 MWh (2-hour) BESS, Munmorah/Tomago, Hunter Valley NSW. Developer: Akaysha Energy (BlackRock Real Assets). Technology: Powin Centipede Energy Segment (LFP, CATL cells). Inverter: EKS Energy (Hitachi Energy) PCS x288. EPC: CPP. Three Wilson Transformer 350 MVA units (HVT1/HVT2/HVT3). NSW SIPS contract ~$705M/5yr via TransGrid. HVT3 catastrophic failure Oct 2025 — operating at 350 MW (41%) on HVT1 only.

capacity_mw.note: Design/registered capacity — actual operational capacity 350 MW since Oct 2025 transformer failure
Derated to 41% of design capacity after HVT3 failure; HVT2 de-energised

storage_mwh.note: Only ~700 MWh accessible via single transformer

capex_aud_m.note: SIPS revenue contract, not capex — but indicates project economics underwrite

bess_oem.note: Powin assets acquired by FlexGen after bankruptcy — warranty and service continuity TBC

status.note: Registered as operating but at 350 MW (41%) since Oct 2025 transformer failure
De facto partial operations — AEMO still shows 'operating' at full registered capacity

cod_current.note: Contingent on HVT3 replacement delivery Q3 2026 and successful commissioning
